nodes.name: 脊索动物门
nodes.summary: 脊索动物门（Chordata）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 乌拉圭
nodes.summary: 乌拉圭是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联186种鸟类，包括大美洲鸵、斑拟䳍、冠叫鸭等186种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Dot-winged crake
nodes.summary: The dot-winged crake (Laterallus spiloptera) is a near threatened species of bird in subfamily Rallinae of family Rallidae, the rails, gallinules, and coots. It is found in Argentina, Brazil, Chile, and Uruguay. Taxonomy and systematics The dot-winged crake was originally described in genus Porzana. However, phylogenetic analyses of mitochondrial DNA by Garcia et al. (2014) placed it in the predominantly South American clade Laterallus. Stervander et al. (2019) demonstrated that it is the sister species of the world's smallest flightless bird, the Inaccessible Island rail Laterallus rogersi (previously placed in the monotypic genus Atlantisia) and should bear the binomial Laterallus spilopterus, which was supported by Kirchman et al. (2021). As of late 2022, the International Ornithological Committee (IOC) calls the dot-winged crake Laterallus spiloptera, BirdLife International's Handbook of the Birds of the World calls it Laterallus spilopterus, and the Clements taxonomy and the South American Classification Committee of the American Ornithological Society (SACC) call it Porzana spiloptera. The worldwide taxonomic systems agree that the species is monotypic. Description The dot-winged crake is 14 to 15 cm (5.5 to 5.9 in) long. The sexes are alike. Their upperparts are dark olive brown with blackish streaks and some white markings on the flight feathers. Their face and breast are dark gray and their vent area and undertail coverts are barred black and white. Distribution and habitat The dot-winged crake's distribution is unsettled. The IOC and Clements place it in southern Uruguay and northern Argentina. The Cornell Lab of Ornithology's Birds of the World adds Brazil's southernmost state, Rio Grande do Sul, and the SACC adds Chile to those three countries. Cornell's eBird has records in all four countries. The dot-winged crake inhabits freshwater and brackish waters and some drier landscapes as well. It is found in freshwater and tidal marshes, swamps, wet meadows, grasslands, and riparian scrub. Behavior Movement No movements are known for the dot-winged crake. Feeding The dot-winged crake feeds on insects, seeds, and marsh vegetation. Its foraging technique has not been documented. Breeding A dot-winged crake nest was discovered near Buenos Aires but no details of it or any other aspects of the species' breeding biology are known. Vocalization The dot-winged crake's song is " a high note followed by a lower-pitched rattle 'kee-krrrrr'" that is repeated several times. Its calls include "a mellow soft 'pwup' and a scolding rattle." Status The IUCN originally assessed the dot-winged crake as Threatened. In 1994 the assessment was updated to Vulnerable, and again to Near Threatened in 2025. Its range is small and fragmented, and its estimated population of 2500 to 10,000 mature individuals is believed to be decreasing. Conversion of wetlands to agriculture, especially grazing with accompanying burning, appears to be the main threat. Other wetlands have been flooded. It occurs in a few protected areas but "[d]istributional surveys [are] urgently needed within [the] species' limited range". References

nodes.name: 田雞屬
nodes.summary: 田雞屬（Porzana）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联6种鸟类，如Ash-throated crake、斑胸田鸡、Australian crake等6种鸟类。

nodes.name: Dot-winged crake
nodes.summary: Dot-winged crake（Porzana spiloptera）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联1种鸟类，如Dot-winged crake。

nodes.name: 稀树草原
nodes.summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

nodes.name: 鹤形目
nodes.summary: 鹤形目（Gruiformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联126种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、灰冠鹤、Grey-winged trumpeter等126种鸟类。

nodes.name: 秧鸡科
nodes.summary: 秧鸡科（Rallidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联103种鸟类，如Tsingy forest rail、Madagascar forest rail、Chestnut forest rail等103种鸟类。

nodes.name: NT
nodes.summary: NT表示 IUCN 近危等级，当前关联914种鸟类，如帝企鹅、小斑几维鸟、大美洲鸵等914种鸟类。

nodes.name: 沼泽
nodes.summary: 具有较高含水量和密生植被的浅水环境。
